panels.title: Allocatable CPU (cores)
panels.description: Shows the total allocatable CPU cores across all Kubernetes nodes. Compare against requested CPU to gauge cluster headroom.

panels.title: Allocatable Memory
panels.description: Shows the total allocatable memory across all Kubernetes nodes. Low headroom may prevent new pod scheduling.

panels.title: Container CPU Time
panels.description: Tracks CPU time consumed by each container over time. Containers consistently near their CPU limits are candidates for throttling.

panels.title: CPU Throttling Time
panels.description: Shows the time containers spent being CPU-throttled by the kernel. Non-zero throttling means containers are hitting their CPU limits.

panels.title: Container Memory Usage
panels.description: Tracks memory consumption per container over time. Containers approaching their memory limits risk being OOM-killed.

panels.title: OOM Kill Count
panels.description: Shows the number of out-of-memory kills per container. Any OOM kills indicate that memory limits need to be increased or the application has a leak.

panels.title: Pod Phase Distribution
panels.description: Displays the distribution of pods across phases such as Running, Pending, and Failed. A high Pending count suggests scheduling or resource constraints.

panels.title: ReplicaSet Status (Desired vs Available)
panels.description: Compares desired versus available replicas for each deployment. A mismatch indicates pods that failed to schedule or start.

panels.title: Pod Restarts
panels.description: Lists pods ranked by restart count. Frequent restarts point to crash loops, health-check failures, or resource limits being exceeded.

panels.title: Volume Usage
panels.description: Shows persistent volume capacity and current usage per claim. Volumes nearing full capacity may cause application write failures.
